assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes notifications into types including 'Security patch available', 'Auto-upgrade scheduled', 'Version available', 'End-of-life warning', and additional types like 'Deprecated API detected'

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response includes an Action Matrix table that assigns urgency levels: Security patches = HIGH, Auto-upgrade scheduled = MEDIUM, Version available = LOW, End-of-life = HIGH/CRITICAL

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states end-of-life warnings require action with timeline-based responses: '90+ days: Add to quarterly planning', '30-60 days: HIGH priority - begin upgrade planning', etc., and emphasizes planning upgrades before support ends

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response provides decision criteria for auto-upgrade scheduled notifications including checking for deployment freezes, critical business events, and insufficient testing, plus commands for deferring if needed

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response states security patches require action within 7-14 days (sooner for critical CVEs) and includes them in escalation matrix with security team ownership, implying expedited timelines

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response explicitly categorizes 'Version available' as LOW urgency with 'Plan for next cycle' action and states 'PLAN FOR NEXT CYCLE • Add to upgrade backlog • No immediate action required'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands for setting up Pub/Sub notifications and provides Cloud Logging queries for different notification types, plus mentions setting up alert routing by notification type

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Review CVE details in GKE release notes' for security patches and 'Review breaking changes in release notes' for version available notifications
